candidates.title: Collapse the Order intake pipeline
candidates.problem: Order intake module is shallow — interface nearly matches the implementation.
candidates.solution: Deepen: one interface, one place to test.
wins: locality: bugs concentrate in one module
leverage: one interface, N call sites

diagram.before:
flowchart LR
  A[OrderHandler] --> B[OrderValidator]
  B --> C[OrderEnricher]
  C --> D[OrderPersister]

candidates.title: Promote PricingPort to a real seam
candidates.problem: Pricing leaks across the seam mid-business-logic.
candidates.solution: Two adapters justify the seam: HTTP in prod, in-memory in tests.
wins: interface shrinks; implementation absorbs the wrappers

diagram.before:
flowchart LR
  I[OrderIntake] -.leak.-> P[PricingClient]
  classDef leak stroke:#dc2626,stroke-width:2px;
  class P leak
diagram.after:
flowchart LR
  I[OrderIntake] --> Port((PricingPort))
  Port --> H[HttpPricingAdapter]
  Port --> M[InMemoryPricingAdapter]

candidates.adr_callout: Contradicts ADR-0007 — worth reopening because the seam is now untested.

top_recommendation.why: Highest locality gain, no ADR conflict, no cross-seam dependencies.
